place: Oakhurst CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Oakhurst’s name comes from a once‑abundant grove of oak trees that once shaded the area.
The 1700s Eden Woolley House, a Greek Revival gem, now houses the Township of Ocean Historical Museum in Joe Palaia Park after its 2005 relocation.
Mid‑18th‑century Quakers and Baptists settled Oakhurst, then called Oceanville, seeking religious freedom along the Monmouth Road, now the Mechanicsville‑Oceanville Turnpike.
The Oakhurst Inn, founded in the mid‑19th century, grew into a motorist haven as Route 35 hotels sprang up to serve Jersey Shore travelers.
Poplar Brook, the community’s main drainage, flows eastward to the Atlantic at Deal, linking Oakhurst to the ocean.
Oakhurst’s terrain rises from under 20 feet along Poplar Brook to over 60 feet in its northern and southwestern corners.
